negative_variants.description: Laplace Nd Lowpass Filter: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Apply the declared laplace_nd transfer function to V(in).

Laplace Nd Lowpass Filter: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use laplace_nd() for continuous-time transfer modeling fail.
Laplace Nd Lowpass Filter: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Laplace Nd Lowpass Filter: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Apply the declared laplace_nd transfer function to V(in) fails.

Laplace Nd Lowpass Filter: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use laplace_nd() for continuous-time transfer modeling fail.
